vocab.example: In my early teens, I became obsessed with collecting different succulent varieties due to their geometric shapes and resilience.
I remember checking the pots every morning, eagerly waiting for the germination process to begin so I could see the first sprouts.
I tried to grow ferns in a shady corner of our balcony, as they couldn't withstand the direct midday sun.
My grandfather passed down his botanical knowledge to me, teaching me which plants required more drainage than others.

vocab.meaning: nở rộ (hoa nở đồng loạt và rực rỡ)
vocab.example: It was a magical moment when the rose bushes I had watered all spring finally burst into bloom in early June.
While my friends liked flowers, I preferred foliage plants with variegated leaves that added texture to our small garden.
We avoided chemicals and only used organic fertilizer made from kitchen scraps to nourish our vegetable patch.
I felt so discouraged when my delicate seedlings started to wilt under the heat of an intense summer afternoon.
Our apartment was filled with potted greenery, mostly herbs that I had raised from small cuttings given by neighbors.
Growing my own peppers as a child helped me develop basic horticultural skills that I still use today.
